title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 101:1
body: The Clattering Blow the Resurrection that will make hearts clatter by its terrors.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 101:10
body: And what will show you what it is? that is to say what the Abyss is.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 101:11
body: It is A scorching fire of extremely hot temperature the hā’ of hiya is for consonantal quiescence and is retained when reciting without a subsequent pause or with; some omit it when reciting without a pause.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 101:2
body: What is the Clattering Blow? — intended to emphasise its awesomeness mā’l-qāri‘a both of these elements constitute a subject and a predicate and together the predicate of the first al-qāri‘a.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 101:3
body: And what will show you what will make known to you what the Clattering Blow is? — additional emphasis of its awesomeness the first mā is a subject and what follows it is its predicate; the second mā and its predicate also function together as the second direct object of the verb adrā ‘show’.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 101:4
body: The day yawma that which renders it accusative is the verb indicated by al-qāri‘a in other words by the implied taqra‘u ‘it clatters’ mankind will be like scattered moths like a throng of scattered locusts surging into each other in confusion until they are summoned to the Reckoning

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 101:5
body: and the mountains will be like tufts of wool like carded wool in terms of the lightness with which it floats in the air until it comes to settle upon the earth.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 101:6
body: Then as for him whose scales weigh heavy in that his good deeds outweigh his misdeeds

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 101:7
body: he will enjoy a pleasant life in Paradise that is to say a pleasing one for he will be pleased with it that is it will be pleasing to him;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 101:8
body: but as for him whose scales weigh light in that his evil deeds outweigh his good ones

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 101:9
body: his home will be the Abyss.
